message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Driver rotation: Luca is Driver for Task #11">
Driver rotation complete. We're now working on Task #11: Implementation Step 1 — Navigation + Dashboard skeleton.

**Luca Palmieri** is the new Driver (the only agent who may modify files).
**Marty Cagan** has been respawned as a Reviewer.

Luca will be implementing US-GTD-8 from docs/user-stories.md. Please review his work as it progresses and send feedback to luca-palmieri from your area of expertise. Key areas to watch:
- Kent: TDD discipline, test-first approach
- Scott: Domain types for routes and navigation state
- Carson: HTMX patterns, hx-boost, route structure
- Lea: CSS for header nav, flexbox wrap, design tokens
- Steve Schoger: Visual hierarchy, spacing, nav design
- Steve Krug: Usability of navigation, trunk test
- Heydon: Nav landmark, aria-current, keyboard nav, skip link
- Marty: Match against US-GTD-8 acceptance criteria
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good -- we're moving to implementation. Let me review what Luca needs from a design perspective for the navigation + dashboard skeleton, and send him my design specs proactively.

input.content:
Luca, Steve Schoger here. Welcome to the Driver seat. Here are the design specs you need for the navigation + dashboard implementation (US-GTD-8). This is everything the team aligned on this session. ## Header Nav Design Spec **Component name:** `.gtd-header` **Structure (desktop):** ``` [Brand] Inbox(3) Next Actions Projects Waiting For Someday Review [____Capture____] Sign out ``` **Structure (mobile, below 1024px):** ``` [Brand] [+] Sign out Inbox(3) Next Actions Projects Waiting For Someday Review ``` **HTML pattern:** ```html
Todo List
Inbox (3) Next Actions Projects Waiting For Someday/Maybe Weekly Review
``` **CSS specs (all values reference existing tokens):** Nav items: - Font: `--font-size-sm` - Default: `color: var(--color-fg-muted)`, `font-weight: var(--font-medium)` - Hover: `color: var(--color-fg)` - Active (`[aria-current="page"]`): `color: var(--color-fg)`, `font-weight: var(--font-semibold)`, `border-block-end: 3px solid var(--color-primary)` - ALL items get `border-block-end: 3px solid transparent` by default (prevents layout shift) - Text decoration: `none` (these are nav items, not inline links) Tier weighting (visual hierarchy): - Tier 1 (Inbox, Next Actions, Projects): `--font-semibold` (600) - Tier 2 (Waiting For, Someday/Maybe, Review): `--font-normal` (400) - Same color for both tiers -- hierarchy through weight only Layout: - Flexbox: `display: flex`, `flex-wrap: wrap`, `align-items: center` - Gap: `--space-md` (16px) desktop, `--space-sm` (8px) mobile - Brand: `font-weight: var(--font-semibold)`, `color: var(--color-fg)`, no underline - Sign out pushed right: `margin-inline-start: auto` on `.gtd-header__actions` Nav count: - Plain text in parentheses: `(3)` - Wrapped in `` for CSS styling - Style: `font-size: var(--font-size-xs)`, `color: var(--color-fg-muted)` - Zero count: don't render the `` at all ## Dashboard Design Spec **Component name:** `.gtd-dashboard` **Structure:** 4 stat cards in a grid ```html
3 Inbox items 1 Stalled projects 12 Next actions 4 Waiting for
``` CSS specs: - Grid: `display: grid`, `grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr` mobile, `repeat(4, 1fr)` desktop (1024px+) - Gap: `--space-md` - Card: `background: var(--color-bg-raised)`, `padding: var(--space-lg)`, `border-radius: var(--radius-md)`, `box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm)`, `text-align: center`, `text-decoration: none`, `color: var(--color-fg)` - Card hover: `box-shadow: var(--shadow-md)` (subtle lift) - Count: `font-size: var(--font-size-2xl)`, `font-weight: var(--font-bold)`, `line-height: var(--leading-tight)`, `display: block` - Label: `font-size: var(--font-size-sm)`, `color: var(--color-fg-muted)`, `display: block` - Stalled projects card (`--warning` modifier): count in `--color-danger` if > 0. If 0, normal treatment. - All zero counts: show "0", don't hide cards. Users should see the full picture. ## Content Area - Max width bumps to 44rem for GTD pages: use a new `.gtd-content` class (don't modify existing `.container`) - Or just use `.container` for now during skeleton and we refine later ## Important Notes 1. **Auth pages** (login, register, forgot-password) keep their existing minimal header. The `.gtd-header` only appears on authenticated GTD pages. 2. **The quick capture input in the header** can be deferred to the Inbox Capture task (Step 2). For the skeleton, just have the nav links + sign out. 3. **Route skeleton**: The nav links should point to real routes that return placeholder pages. Each placeholder page should have an `

` matching the nav label and a brief "Coming soon" message. This lets us verify navigation works end-to-end. 4. **`hx-boost="true"` on ``** (Carson's recommendation) makes all nav links HTMX-boosted automatically. No special attributes needed on individual links. Let me know if anything needs clarification. I'll be reviewing your CSS as you go.
input.summary: Complete design specs for nav + dashboard implementation

Users should see the full picture. ## Content Area - Max width bumps to 44rem for GTD pages: use a new `.gtd-content` class (don't modify existing `.container`) - Or just use `.container` for now during skeleton and we refine later ## Important Notes 1. **Auth pages** (login, register, forgot-password) keep their existing minimal header. content.text:
Sent Luca complete design specs for the navigation and dashboard implementation, covering:

**Header nav:** Full HTML pattern, CSS specs for all states (default, hover, active), tier weighting, flexbox layout, nav count styling, mobile wrapping behavior

**Dashboard:** 4 stat cards with HTML pattern, CSS grid layout (2x2 mobile, 4-across desktop), card styling with shadow-lift hover, stalled projects warning treatment

**Implementation notes:** Auth pages keep minimal header, quick capture deferred to Step 2, route skeleton with placeholder pages, `hx-boost="true"` on body

Now watching for Luca's implementation to review CSS and visual hierarchy as he works.
